headline: AI demand fuels investor portfolios while oil posts biggest monthly decline
detail: This is the direct macro backdrop for your book today. AI spending narrative keeps semiconductor and mega-cap tech bid, directly supporting SMH, SOXL, CRWD, and QQQ. Oil's decline removes an inflation re-acceleration fear, which is mildly positive for growth multiples.
rec: No specific new buy warranted - this headline confirms your existing holdings are in the right sector. Just let the positions run above their stops; no need to chase here.

headline: Anthropic says Trump admin lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
detail: Export control relaxation on AI models is incrementally positive for the AI infrastructure chain - chip designers and fab equipment names benefit when the regulatory ceiling lifts. This could support NVDA, AMAT, and by extension SMH and SOXL in your book.
rec: NVDA has a scanner setup (entry 198.62-198.39, stop 197.35) but scores only 7/12 with a grade C bias - the news is supportive but not strong enough alone to add margin exposure. Watch how NVDA reacts at that entry zone before committing.

headline: Japan spent $74 billion propping up the yen - real battle is with the Fed
detail: If yen intervention fails and the dollar surges, risk appetite can compress quickly and growth/tech multiples get hit hardest - exactly your concentrated book. This is not a today-problem necessarily, but it is the biggest macro tail risk sitting under your positions.
rec: No trade now, but this is the reason you do not get complacent on stops. If you see yen volatility spike suddenly, your leveraged names SOXL and SPAL are the first to cut.

headline: LNG market disruption may continue for months as top producer withholds Italian shipments
detail: Energy supply disruption is a mild inflation risk and could pressure Europe's economy - relevant because European demand matters for global chip end-markets, especially industrial semis. Not an immediate threat to your book but worth noting if it escalates.
rec: Too indirect to trigger a trade in your tech/chip-focused book today. No action needed.
